title: Chapter 2592 - 51: Crimson Sun
content:
Chapter 2592: Chapter 51: Crimson Sun

The big tiger transformed into black and white colors, carrying Mo Hua, and charged into the night.

Inside the camp of the Great Wilderness Sect.

Young Master Tuoba lay on the ground, his chest bloody.

Beside him, the Golden Core Elders hurriedly took out pills and spiritual liquid to stop Young Master Tuoba’s bleeding and heal his wounds, feeling fearful inside. If not for the Sect Leader’s Spirit Tool Heart Protecting Mirror, Young Master Tuoba might truly have been crippled by the ferocious tiger demon.

A moment later, the bleeding from Young Master Tuoba’s chest stopped, the wound stinging and burning.

But the pain from the wound was far less than the jealousy, anger, and agony in his heart.

As a member of the Great Wilderness Royal Family, despite his painstaking efforts, using every means, continuously training, suppressing, and tormenting, he failed to tame the king beast tiger demon. Yet now, right in front of him, it voluntarily lowered the head of a king, submitting to others, willingly becoming someone else’s mount.

This was simply more unbearable than killing him.

"This deep hatred is irreconcilable!" Young Master Tuoba gritted his teeth and said, "Quickly! Go! Issue the order, block the entire line of the Great Wilderness Sect, kill that kid surnamed Mo, and bring back that king beast!"

"Yes, Young Master!"

A crowd of Golden Core Elders all bowed their hands in agreement.

They also understood in their hearts that this was no small matter.

Black and white monster qi is rarely seen in the world, this black and white fierce tiger is definitely an anomaly among tigers, a treasure among king beasts.

Only such a king beast is worthy of the Young Master’s status.

Additionally, this demon is fierce in momentum and astounding in strength. Currently, as a second grade creature it’s already so ferocious—once it reaches third grade and forms a true demon core, its strength will greatly advance, making it even harder to tame then.

And that young man... What kind of person is he exactly?

Clearly his cultivation is unremarkable, and his bloodline doesn’t seem that noble, so why can he make such a fierce king beast voluntarily submit?

No chains, no saddle, no formations, there isn’t any suppression or restraint involved.

For a tiger demon to voluntarily submit, it’s simply incredible.

Monster beasts eat people and their fierce nature is hard to extinguish.

Generally, monster beasts can’t truly be "tamed."

Taming monster beasts essentially only involves forceful "suppression" using bloodline and power, making them obey.

Once a monster beast breaks free from restraints, it still might "devour its master."

Therefore, generally "taming monsters" must employ saddles, monster locks, and Demon Binding Formation to balance the monster beast.

Without restraints, even making an ordinary monster submit is difficult, let alone a ferocious tiger as a king of demons.

Thus, this young man who makes the king beast voluntarily bow and submit is indeed quite ominous.

Could it be...he isn’t actually human?

This suspicion rose in the minds of the cultivators from the Great Wilderness Sect.

But at this moment, they couldn’t delve deeply into it; the urgent task was still intercepting the tiger demon.

This young man must die!

And the black and white marked tiger beast must be reclaimed!

The Golden Core Elders of the Great Wilderness Sect issued orders with the Great Wilderness Order to all Great Wilderness Sect cultivators:

"All disciples preparing for battle from the Great Wilderness Sect, blockade the front line, surround a youth riding a black and white tiger demon."

"Utilize military formations to intercept the tiger demon, capture that young man. If there is resistance, kill without mercy!"

The orders were given, and the Great Wilderness Sect’s lines changed accordingly.

Most of the cultivators from the Great Wilderness Sect were still engaged in chaotic battles with the Taoist Court.

A small portion of those preparing for battle had already started to turn their spears towards Mo Hua.

This was a large-scale combat; even if it’s just a small portion, the numbers were quite large.

Before long, riding on the big tiger’s back, Mo Hua immediately felt the pressure increase immensely.

The big tiger’s talent allows it to release black and white monster qi; its own fur color changes with it, confuses the energy, hides killing intent, and increases speed. Whether for ambush, slaughter, or escape, it’s very powerful.

Mo Hua was witnessing it for the first time too.

But this talent doesn’t last long. After leaving the camp and escaping from the pursuit of the Great Wilderness Sect, the black and white colors on the big tiger’s body returned to normalcy.

It could only, as usual, carry Mo Hua and break through the defense line of the Great Wilderness Sect.

And under the current circumstances, breaking through is actually the most difficult part.

This path of breakout, when Mo Hua came alone, invisible, was already filled with crises at every step.

Now the big tiger, being such a large target, trying to charge out of the Great Wilderness Sect’s position and return by the original route, naturally faces even greater dangers.

The only good news is that Feather Transformation has not arrived.

The Feather Transformation of the Great Wilderness Sect seems to be entangled with the Feather Transformation of the Taoist Court, so it hasn’t come to chase and kill.

And Young Master Tuoba’s life is also not in serious danger.

Feather Transformation of the Great Wilderness Sect, more unlikely to disregard the broader situation to target Mo Hua as a "little shrimp."

But under Young Master Tuoba’s orders, more and more cultivators from the Great Wilderness Sect began to surround Mo Hua.

Within these cultivators, there were scattered Golden Cores, most were Foundation Establishments.

The threat from Golden Cores was relatively manageable because the battlefield is spacious, the battle lines are stretched very long; Golden Cores from the Great Wilderness Sect are dispersed across various places, leading their teams in chaotic battles with the Taoist Court.

Thus, after Mo Hua broke out, he didn’t encounter many Golden Cores along the way.

Instead, the many Foundation Establishment disciples of the Great Wilderness Sect formed an array, making it difficult to deal with.

These disciples were originally trained according to the formation of "Taoist Soldiers."

They were originally supposed to follow the Taoist Soldiers Court together to put down the rebellion at the Great Wilderness.

Therefore, armor, spiritual artifacts, formations, formation arrays, tactics for battle—none were without meeting the requirements of Taoist Soldiers.

Once they formed Taoist Soldiers, these well-trained Foundation Establishments of the Great Wilderness Sect were actually even harder to deal with than the Golden Cores.
